title: chaenomeles × superba f. rosea
intro: Chaenomeles × superba f. rosea, also known as the double-flowered flowering quince, is a hybrid plant species within the Rosaceae family, characterized by its vibrant pink to red, double-petaled flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation due to its high content of flavonoids and anthocyanins. The health benefits of Chaenomeles × superba f. rosea include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds and fever, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
